world.title: Shabana Mahmood does not rule out sending back refused Afghan asylum seekers

world.title: Mall of Louisiana shooting: 1 arrested after shooting that killed teen girl

world.title: Indonesia Shuts Down Malacca Strait Toll Talk, Distancing From Hormuz

world.title: Africa calls for urgent climate action

world.title: Italian-born Bishop Feted for Promoting Peace in South Sudan, Calls for Renewed Solidarity with Embattled Communities
world.description: Bishop Christian Carlassare of South Sudan’s  Catholic Diocese of Bentiu has been awarded the prestigious Giorgio La Pira Prize, for his commitment to peace in the embattled east-central African country.

world.title: Wounded Lebanese journalist recounts hours of agony when Israeli strike killed colleague

world.title: Taiwan Fears It’ll Be ‘On the Menu’ at Xi’s Summit With Trump

business.title: Could nuclear power solve the Yukon’s energy problem?

ai.title: OpenAI’s Sam Altman apologizes to Canadian community after failing to flag mass shooter’s conversations with its AI chatbot
ai.description: Sam Altman, the head of OpenAI, has formally apologized to the community of Tumbler Ridge, BC, after a mass shooting in February. He admitted his company did not alert authorities to the shooter’s disturbing online conversations with its AI chatbot even after staff flagged the account internally.
